title: Drawdown Solutions Explorer
description: The 100 most substantive solutions to reverse global warming — ranked, compared, and scored by impact.

stages.title: 100 Solutions That Can Reverse Global Warming
stages.body:
Drawdown is the point at which greenhouse gases in the atmosphere stop climbing and start to decline. According to **Project Drawdown**, there are **100+ viable solutions** across every sector that could achieve this.

These aren't pie-in-the-sky ideas — they're **existing technologies and practices** that are already being deployed. The question isn't whether solutions exist. It's whether we can deploy them **fast enough and at scale**.

The top 5 solutions by potential impact:
1. 🌾 Reduced food waste — **87.45 GtCO₂-equivalent**
2. 🌳 Onshore wind turbines — **147.7 GtCO₂**
3. 🌊 Offshore wind turbines — **139.7 GtCO₂**
4. 🏠 Improved refrigeration management — **89.74 GtCO₂**
5. 🌱 Tropical forest restoration — **83.56 GtCO₂**

stages.title: Solution Spotlight
stages.description: Explore the top drawdown solutions. Filter by sector.

cards.title: Onshore Wind Turbines
cards.subtitle: Energy Sector
cards.detail: Wind turbines on land. Reduces CO₂ by avoiding fossil fuel electricity generation. Cost: $36/ton CO₂ avoided. Creates local jobs and energy independence.

cards.title: Reduced Food Waste
cards.subtitle: Food & Agriculture Sector
cards.detail: One-third of all food produced is lost or wasted. Reducing this by 50% by 2050 could avoid 87.45 GtCO₂-equivalent. Requires cold chains, better storage, and consumer education.

cards.title: Plant-Rich Diets
cards.subtitle: Food & Agriculture Sector
cards.detail: Shifting toward plant-based diets reduces methane from livestock and land-use change. Without giving up meat entirely, even moderate shifts have massive impact.

cards.title: Tropical Forest Restoration
cards.subtitle: Land Use Sector
cards.detail: Restoring degraded tropical forests to capture carbon, protect biodiversity, and regulate water cycles. Two billion hectares of land are suitable for restoration.

cards.title: Rooftop Solar
cards.subtitle: Buildings & Cities Sector
cards.detail: Distributed solar panels on rooftops. Reduces transmission losses, democratizes energy, and lowers electricity costs for households.

cards.title: Electric Vehicles
cards.subtitle: Transport Sector
cards.detail: Replacing internal combustion engines with electric vehicles powered by clean electricity. Battery costs fell 97% since 1991.

stages.title: What If We Deployed Solutions Faster?
stages.description: Adjust deployment speed to see impact on atmospheric CO₂ by 2050.
stages.resultTemplate:
Projected CO₂ reduction by 2050: {{result}} GtCO₂-eq
Business-as-usual trajectory: +800 GtCO₂-eq by 2050

questions.question: What is the #1 ranked drawdown solution?
questions.explanation: Onshore wind turbines are ranked #1 by Project Drawdown with 147.7 GtCO₂ reduction potential.

questions.question: How much food is wasted globally?
questions.explanation: Approximately one-third of all food produced globally is lost or wasted.

questions.question: What is 'drawdown'?
options: The point when CO₂ levels stop rising and begin to decline
questions.explanation: Drawdown is the future point in time when levels of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere stop climbing and start to steadily decline.

questions.question: Which sector contributes the most drawdown solutions?
questions.explanation: The Energy sector contains the most high-impact solutions including wind, solar, and nuclear.

stages.title: Solutions Exist. Personal Action Completes the Picture.
stages.body:
You've explored **100+ solutions** that can reverse global warming. The science is clear, the technology exists, and the economics increasingly favor action.

But systemic change starts with **personal understanding** — knowing your own carbon footprint and where you can make the biggest difference.

**How big is YOUR footprint?**
